title: NVIDIA argues open synthetic data is becoming core infrastructure for agents
friendlyTitle: NVIDIA says better agents need better open data
summary: NVIDIA published a Hugging Face post on why agentic AI needs open data, including software-engineering traces, tool-use failures, retrieval patterns, safety data, workflow execution, and user simulation. The post highlights Nemotron data releases, synthetic data, and an interactive Prompt Atlas for exploring post-training samples.
friendlySummary: NVIDIA explained why AI agents need data about tool use, mistakes, workflows, and safety — not just bigger models.
whyItMatters: Better agents need more than model weights: they need inspectable examples of tool use, failure recovery, and workflow behavior. Open synthetic data can help teams build and audit agents without exposing proprietary workflows or private customer data.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because companies want useful AI without leaking private information. Synthetic open data can help models improve while keeping secrets safer.
